subject: Cloud Computing
title: SaaS Sprawl: Too Many Tools
description: A 60-second lesson on SaaS Sprawl: Too Many Tools.

hook.text: The average enterprise pays for 300+ SaaS subscriptions. Many are duplicates nobody tracks.

buildup.text: SaaS sprawl happens when departments buy tools independently without central coordination.

discovery.text: Shadow IT — tools adopted without IT approval — accounts for 40% of SaaS spending in many companies.

twist.text: Unused licenses waste billions globally. Studies show 25% of SaaS licenses go unused each month.

climax.text: SaaS management platforms now exist just to track and optimize all your other SaaS subscriptions.

punchline.text: There's now SaaS to manage your SaaS problem. Oh, the irony.

quiz.question: What is 'SaaS sprawl'?
options: Uncontrolled adoption of too many SaaS tools with overlapping functionality
A SaaS application that grows too large to function properly
When a SaaS company expands to too many countries
